commits.message:
mit-cut-plan-lsh.1: motion grammar — one named timing authority (#10)

* mit-cut-plan-lsh.1: motion grammar — one named timing authority

Mint the page's motion vocabulary once and route every scene through it,
replacing the scattered easing/duration literals the facets each invented.

- Easing families + duration scale as CSS :root tokens (--ease-land/-fade,
  --t-tap/-ctl/-veil/-shift, --dur-bob/-blink). Every inline cubic-bezier and
  transition/animation duration now references a token; the only remaining
  literal is the reduced-motion .001ms kill switch (a deliberate exception).
- One source of truth across the CSS/JS divide: the JS MOTION object READS the
  two curve strings WAAPI needs from the CSS tokens (getComputedStyle), so a
  curve is one fact with no twin to drift. The scroll-scrub easing (drift) is
  JS-only and authored once as the exact cubic.  [LAW:one-source-of-truth]
- Read-beat grammar: the compositional invariant bmf asked for — wording holds
  still for an opening beat, then animates; ambient motion may still breathe.
  The INVARIANT is fixed; the LENGTH is a per-scene value from a sanctioned
  palette (brisk/read/dwell). A node names its beat as data; the one paint loop
  turns it into n.w (wording progress) that every painter draws its text from,
  while raw p drives the ambient channel.
  [LAW:dataflow-not-control-flow] [LAW:one-type-per-behavior]

Verified in-browser (chrome-devtools): full-page scroll sweep with zero runtime
errors; grammar mirror resolves (drift 0/.875/1); beats vary across scenes
(dwell 6 / read 19 / brisk 6); the opener's wording holds through its dwell beat
(label "1" until p>.26) then advances, with the final state unchanged.

* mit-cut-plan-lsh.1: fail loud when the motion grammar is misconfigured

Two silent-failure holes in the new grammar, closed at their single enforcers:

- cssVar() returned '' for a missing CSS token, which would flow into WAAPI as
  an invalid easing string — the animation dies with no error. Now it throws a
  self-describing error the moment the JS↔CSS mirror is broken (a renamed or
  dropped token), covering both WAAPI call sites from one place.
- A misspelled read-beat (e.g. beat:'dwelll') silently fell back to 'read', so
  a scene's timing would lie with no signal. buildNode now validates the beat
  the same way it already validates the painter kind — undefined stays the
  authorised default, a typo fails loudly at construction.

[LAW:no-silent-failure] [LAW:single-enforcer]

* mit-cut-plan-lsh.1: bring the WAAPI flourish durations into the CSS inventory

The :root comment claimed every duration the design uses is named in CSS, but
the two WAAPI flourish lengths (240ms text crossfade, 430ms scaleword pop) lived
as JS literals in MOTION.ms — making the claim a half-truth and leaving those
values outside the single guarded mirror.

Make the claim mechanically true rather than weakening it: --ms-fade/--ms-pop
now live in :root and are read into MOTION.ms through cssMs(), a numeric variant
of the same enforcer (missing or non-numeric token fails loud). The CSS :root is
now the genuine single inventory of all design timing.

[LAW:one-source-of-truth] [LAW:single-enforcer] [LAW:comments-explain-why-only]

* mit-cut-plan-lsh.1: declare the read-beat's scope so exemptions aren't silent

~22 dynamic-text painters draw from n.w (read-beat-applied), but a few use raw p
on purpose — and that choice was invisible, indistinguishable from a missed
conversion. The loop comment also over-claimed ("every painter draws its TEXT
from n.w"), which is no longer true.

State the scope once, at the single place n.w is computed: text that ANIMATES IN
uses n.w; text that is a LIVE READOUT of the ambient channel (runway's pin-phase
label, waffle's %) or is ITSELF the ambient graphic (takeover swell, horizontal
filmstrip) tracks raw p by design — gating it would desync the label from what it
reports. Pointer comments at the two non-obvious callsites (runway, waffle).

No behavior change. [LAW:comments-explain-why-only] [LAW:single-enforcer]

* mit-cut-plan-lsh.1: readout captions track their visual, not the read-beat

The read-beat was over-applied: five captions that REPORT a continuously-p-driven
visual were gated on n.w, so the label could contradict what's on screen —
"background holds still" while layers visibly drift, a step caption naming a bar
that hasn't started filling, a step label disagreeing with the lit chip.

Test that decides p vs n.w: does the caption make a state-claim a continuous
visual can visibly contradict? If yes, it tracks the visual's progress, not the
read-beat. Reverted to raw p (raw e for the observer's chip index):
- trigger: caption index ↔ lit chip
- chart:   caption ↔ the bar currently drawing
- parallax: caption ↔ layer drift (now flips at the visual midpoint, was p≈.58)
- explode: caption ↔ slab separation
- bind:    caption ↔ the energizing wire

The read-beat stays on narrative wording with no competing visual indicator
(zoom/gauge/ring step-prose, the appearing titles). Removed the now-meaningless
beat on explode/bind so the data doesn't imply a read-beat those scenes can't have.

[LAW:one-source-of-truth] (two indicators of one fact can't disagree)
[LAW:comments-explain-why-only] (each exemption now states why at the callsite)
